author: OE-GOD
name: NN + Byte-Level PPM Adaptive-λ Mixture (non-record demonstration)
val_bpb_measurement_note: val_bpb computed on the first 5,000,000 tokens of validation (12,120,410 bytes) rather than full val — pure-Python PPM is too slow for full val inside the eval budget; this is a non-record demonstration of the lever.
technique_summary: SP1024 + 9L baseline + byte-level PPM-D order-5 with adaptive-λ gate on PPM confidence. Mixture computed per-byte in probability space after spreading NN token logprob uniformly across token UTF-8 bytes.

mixture_technique.predictor: byte-level PPM-D order 5 (pure Python, online, legal TTT)
mixture_technique.mixing: adaptive λ gate: λ=0.05 when PPM max-symbol probability > 0.9, else λ=0.9
mixture_technique.byte_marginalization: spread token logprob uniformly across UTF-8 bytes (conserves total bits for NN)

compliance.eval_under_600s_note: Eval exceeds 10 min on full val due to pure-Python PPM; this submission subsamples 5M tokens so eval fits budget. Record-track integration requires a C/Numba PPM port.
compliance.no_ngram_cache_note: Intentionally uses a byte-level online PPM predictor (not a pre-computed n-gram cache in the artifact). PPM trains from scratch during eval on already-scored tokens, which the challenge explicitly permits as legal TTT.
